info.name: VoiceML API (v0.9.1)
info.description:
# VoiceML API — Official Postman Collection

Version: **0.9.1** · Spec: OpenAPI 3.1 · Host: `https://voiceml.voicetel.com`

Twilio-compatible REST surface for voice, SMS, and AMD. Every endpoint is grouped by resource family.

## Auth model

HTTP Basic Auth on all authenticated endpoints:
- **Username** = your AccountSid (`AC` + 32 hex chars)
- **Password** = your per-account API key

The username must match the `:AccountSid` path parameter on account-scoped routes.

Diagnostic endpoints (`/health`, `/openapi.*`) require no authentication.

## Documentation

- API reference: https://voicetel.com/docs/api/v0.7/voiceml/
- Validator: https://voicetel.com/voiceml/validator/
- SDK catalogue: https://voicetel.com/docs/voiceml-sdks/

item.name: Account
item.description: Account-level metadata — fetch / update Account and fetch Balance. Twilio-compatible.

request.description:
**Fetch the account balance**
Returns the account's current balance. VoiceML is operator-billed —
there is no per-tenant runtime balance ledger — so the balance is
reported as a static zero in the account's currency. The response
mirrors Twilio's Balance resource exactly.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `balance.fetch`

**Fetch the authenticated account**
Returns the authenticated account in the Twilio Account shape
(status/type/owner_account_sid/friendly_name/date_created/
date_updated/uri/subresource_uris). The response ADDITIVELY embeds
VoiceML extension keys (name, voice_url, tts_*, stt_*, aws_*,
allow_cidr, created_at/updated_at). The account secret `auth_token`
is never emitted on this surface; rotate it via
`POST /Accounts/{Sid}/RotateKey`. Self-serve — an account may only
read its own record.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `accounts.fetch`

**Update the authenticated account**
Updates the authenticated account. Accepts form-encoded or JSON
bodies; only the fields present in the request are changed
(only-set-fields-touched). `FriendlyName` maps to the Twilio
`friendly_name`; the remaining fields configure VoiceML voice
routing, BYO STT/TTS, AWS recording storage, and the IP allow-list.
Returns the post-update Account resource in the same Twilio shape as
the fetch. Self-serve — an account may only update its own record.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `accounts.update`

item.name: Calls
item.description: Call resources and subresources — originate, update, terminate, recordings, streams, SIPREC, transcriptions, events, notifications, and <Pay> sessions.

query.description: Calls started on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
Calls started strictly before this UTC date/time.
Calls started strictly after this UTC date/time.
Calls ended on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
Calls ended strictly before this UTC date/time.
Calls ended strictly after this UTC date/time.
Zero-based page index.
Page size. Max 1000; default 50.
The page token. This is provided by the API.

request.description:
**List calls**
Paginated list of call records for the given account. Filters
match Twilio: `To`, `From`, `Status`, `ParentCallSid`,
`StartTime` (bare = full UTC day), `StartTime<` (strict upper),
`StartTime>` (strict lower), and the same triple for `EndTime`.
For backwards compatibility the legacy `StartTime>=` / `EndTime<=`
SQL-style operators are also accepted at the handler. Pagination
via `Page` and `PageSize` (max 1000). `EndTime` filters
restrict the result set to terminal-status rows (an in-progress
call has no end time and cannot match an EndTime window).


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `calls.list`

**Create (originate) a call**
Originates an outbound call. The `To` and `From`
numbers are validated and normalised to E.164 against the
authenticated tenant's `phone_regions` list — set per-account in
the admin API. Empty list disables validation; multi-region
tenants accept numbers valid in any listed region.
`MachineDetection` is optional; omitting it skips AMD entirely.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `calls.create`

query.description: Zero-based page index.
Page size. Max 1000; default 50.
The page token. This is provided by the API.

request.description:
**List per-call lifecycle events (compat stub)**
Twilio's fine-grained per-call event stream. VoiceML
delivers equivalent data via `StatusCallback` (initiated /
ringing / answered / completed); this endpoint is mounted
as a stub returning an empty list so SDK helpers iterate
cleanly. The canonical event source is the customer's
`StatusCallback` URL. Page/PageSize are accepted for SDK
ergonomics but have no effect on an empty list.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callEvents.list`

query.description: Zero-based page index.
Page size. Max 1000; default 50.
The page token. This is provided by the API.

Twilio-compatible parameter — 0 = error-level only, 1 =
warning-level + above. Accepted for SDK migration
compatibility; has no effect since VoiceML's per-call
notification stream is unimplemented (this endpoint is a
stub returning an empty list).
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.

request.description:
**List per-call notifications (compat stub)**
Twilio's per-call alert log. VoiceML doesn't track
notifications as a separate stream — relevant operator-side
events flow through metrics + structured logs. This
endpoint is mounted as a stub so SDK helpers don't 404;
the response is always an empty list. Page/PageSize and Log
are accepted for SDK ergonomics but have no effect on an
empty list.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callNotifications.list`

**Fetch a per-call notification (compat stub)**
Per-call notification fetch. VoiceML does not persist a
notification stream — the list endpoint is always empty and
individual fetches return 404.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callNotifications.fetch`

**Start a <Pay> session on an existing call (REST equivalent of `<Pay>` TwiML)**
Initiates a PCI Pay session against the live call leg. Mirrors
the TwiML `<Pay>` verb's parser + state machine; every
attribute below is accepted and validated. The runtime
(Stripe charge / tokenize) is currently gated; until it is
wired, both surfaces accept and log without invoking the
Stripe connector.

Per-tenant BYO is binding: the tenant's account row must have
`pay_enabled = true` AND `stripe_secret_key` set (admin
opt-in). No server-level Stripe key fallback — multi-tenancy
demands no fallback.

`IdempotencyKey` is accepted and persisted on the row for
diagnostic visibility; replay dedup is NOT enforced today.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `payments.create`

**Update a Pay session — Status=complete or Status=cancel**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `payments.update`

query.description: Filter to recordings created on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
Filter to recordings created strictly before this UTC date.

request.description:
**List recordings for one call**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callRecordings.list`

**Start a recording mid-call**
Begins a fresh recording on the live call leg. The new
Recording resource is returned in the 201 response. Distinct
from `Record=true` on `POST /Calls`, which captures from the
start of the call.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callRecordings.create`

**Fetch recording metadata for a specific call (call-scoped form)**
Same JSON resource as `/Recordings/{RecordingSid}` but enforces
the call-scope from the URL path. 404 if the recording exists
but is attached to a different call.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callRecordings.fetch`

**Update a mid-call recording's status**
Applies stop / pause / resume on the live recording.
All three transitions return 200 + the post-mutation
Recording resource. Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that
belongs to a different account returns 404 without leaking
existence.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callRecordings.update`

**Delete a recording (row + local WAV + S3 object when present)**
Removes the memdb row, unlinks the on-disk WAV, and (when
`RECORDING_S3_BUCKET` is configured) issues an S3 DeleteObject
on the archived copy. S3 deletion is best-effort: failure logs
but does not fail the API call (the bucket lifecycle policy is
the long-stop cleanup). Tenant-scoped — cross-tenant DELETE
returns 404 without affecting the row.


Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `callRecordings.delete`

**List active SIPREC sessions for the call**
Returns 0 or 1 entries — only one SIPREC mirror per call leg
is supported. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `siprec.list`

**Start a SIPREC session on the call (REST equivalent of `<Start><Siprec>`)**
Starts a SIPREC mirror against the configured connector
profile on the live call leg. Returns the new Siprec
resource with an SR-prefixed sid.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `siprec.create`

**Fetch a single SIPREC session by sid**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `siprec.fetch`

**Stop a SIPREC session**
Status=stopped hard-stops the recording: VoiceML detaches the
media fork (RTP stops leaving the media server immediately) and
BYEs the SRS leg, then clears its session-tracking entry. Every
recording on the call leg is stopped. The stop is not resumable —
start a new SIPREC session to record again.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `siprec.update`

**List active media streams for the call**
Returns 0 or 1 entries — the runtime supports a single
active media fork per call, matching the slot model on the
VoiceML tracker. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape so SDK auto-paginators round-trip cleanly.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `streams.list`

**Start a media stream on the call (REST equivalent of `<Connect><Stream>`/`<Start><Stream>`)**
Starts a media fork on the live call leg. Equivalent to
`<Connect><Stream>` / `<Start><Stream>` in TwiML. Returns
the new Stream resource with an MZ-prefixed sid.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `streams.create`

**Fetch a single stream by sid**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `streams.fetch`

**Update a media stream — only Status=stopped is meaningful**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `streams.update`

**List active transcriptions for the call**
Returns 0 or 1 entries. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `transcriptions.list`

**Start real-time transcription on the call (REST equivalent of `<Start><Transcription>`)**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `transcriptions.create`

**Fetch a single transcription by sid**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `transcriptions.fetch`

**Update a transcription — only Status=stopped is meaningful**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `transcriptions.update`

**Send a user-defined message on a live call (NOT IMPLEMENTED)**
Twilio's chat-style messages on a live call. Real
message-passing infrastructure (a websocket on the
customer-facing leg) that's out of scope for VoiceML.
Returns 501 with Twilio code 20501. The empty request body
schema is declared so codegen tooling emits a method
signature consistent with Twilio's SDK.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `userDefinedMessages.create`

**Fetch a call**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `calls.fetch`

**Update or terminate a live call**
Three flows on the same endpoint (mirrors Twilio):
  * `Status=completed|canceled` — terminate the call.
  * `Twiml=<inline>` — execute inline TwiML on the live call
    (wins over `Url` when both are present).
  * `Url=…` — fetch new TwiML and execute it on the live call
    (live redirect / hot swap).
If `Status` is present alongside any TwiML source, `Status`
wins. The success response is the full Call resource — same
shape `GET` returns — so SDK deserialisers round-trip.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `calls.update`

**Delete a call record**

Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `calls.delete`

query.description: Filter to conferences with this exact FriendlyName.

Filter to conferences in this lifecycle state. VoiceML conferences are only ever `in-progress` (rooms vanish when the last participant leaves), so filtering by `init` or `completed` returns an empty list.
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML's conference list is a live query with no historical store — accepted but does not narrow the result set.
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.

The page token. This is provided by the API.

request.description:
**List active conferences for the account**
Returns conferences currently active for this tenant. Live
query — no caching, always reflects state at request time.
Conferences appear once a first participant joins (typically
via TwiML `<Dial><Conference>`) and disappear as soon as the
last participant leaves.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferences.list`

Filter to participants with this Hold state.

request.description:
**List participants in a conference**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `participants.list`

**Dial a participant into a conference**
Places an outbound call from `From` to `To` that joins the named
conference on answer via inline TwiML. Returns immediately with
`status=queued` while the leg is ringing.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `participants.create`

**Fetch a single participant**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `participants.fetch`

**Mute / unmute / hold a participant**
At least one of `Muted` or `Hold` must be set. Twilio's
`HoldUrl` (custom hold-music URL) and `AnnounceUrl` are
not yet implemented.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `participants.update`

**Kick a participant out of the conference**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `participants.delete`

request.description:
**List recordings captured by a conference**
Returns recordings created by `<Dial><Conference record="record-from-start">`
verbs that ran in this conference. Pagination envelope same as
`/Recordings`.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferenceRecordings.list`

**Start a recording on a live conference**
Begins a recording on the named conference's mixed audio. The new
Recording resource is returned with `source` =
`StartConferenceRecordingAPI` and `status` = `in-progress`.
Conference recordings are always mono (`channels` = 1).


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferenceRecordings.create`

**Fetch a conference-scoped recording**
Same JSON resource as `/Recordings/{RecordingSid}` but enforces
the conference scope from the URL path. 404 if the recording exists
but is attached to a different conference.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferenceRecordings.fetch`

**Update a conference-scoped recording's status**
Same semantics as `/Calls/{CallSid}/Recordings/{Sid}` but enforces
conference scope from the URL path.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferenceRecordings.update`

**Delete a conference-scoped recording**
Same semantics as `/Recordings/{Sid}` but enforces conference
scope from the URL path.


Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferenceRecordings.delete`

**Fetch a single conference**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferences.fetch`

**End an active conference**
v1 supports only `Status=completed` — kicks every participant
and tears down the room. Twilio's other update fields
(`Announce*`) are not yet implemented.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `conferences.update`

item.name: Messages
item.description: SMS Messages — send, list, fetch, redact / cancel, delete. Message Media — fetch / list / delete the per-message media inventory. Twilio-compatible /Messages REST surface.

request.description:
**List Messages for the account (paged, DateCreated DESC)**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `messages.list`

**Send an SMS or MMS**
Sends `Body` to `To` via the configured SMS provider. When one
or more `MediaUrl` values are supplied the message is an MMS,
dispatched through the tenant's configured MMS provider (#473)
and `Body` becomes optional. When the provider is not configured
the row is still persisted with status=failed and
error_code=21609 so the integrator can introspect the rejection
through GET /Messages/{Sid}.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `messages.create`

(front of queue).

**Fetch a specific member by CallSid**

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

**Dequeue the front member to a TwiML URL**
Pops the longest-waiting member and redirects their call to the

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `queues.update`

Refused with 409 if any members are still waiting — caller must
drain the queue first (matches Twilio's behaviour).


Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `queues.delete`

request.description:
**Fetch recording metadata**
Returns JSON metadata for the recording. The companion
`.wav` path returns the audio bytes (or a 302/410 redirect
depending on archival state); see `recordings.fetchAudio`.

Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that belongs to a different
account returns `404` with the same shape as a nonexistent
sid — no enumeration leak.

See [docs/aws-recordings.md](https://github.com/voicetel/callBroadcast/blob/main/docs/aws-recordings.md)
for the archival pipeline.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `recordings.fetch`

**Delete a recording (row + local WAV + S3 object when present)**
Removes the memdb row, unlinks the on-disk WAV, and (when
`RECORDING_S3_BUCKET` is configured) issues an S3 DeleteObject
on the archived copy. S3 deletion is best-effort: failure logs
but does not fail the API call (the bucket lifecycle policy is
the long-stop cleanup). Tenant-scoped — cross-tenant DELETE
returns 404 without affecting the row.


Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `recordings.delete`

**Fetch recording audio (WAV)**
Returns the captured audio as `audio/wav` with one of three
delivery shapes:

  * `200 OK` — local file present (typical for the first
    `RECORDING_LOCAL_GRACE` window after the call ends).
  * `302 Found` — local file cleaned up but archived to S3;
    `Location` header points at a presigned URL with TTL
    `RECORDING_PRESIGN_TTL`.
  * `410 Gone` — local file gone AND no `s3_key` set
    (typically: archival not configured + cleanup ran, or
    archival failed and the WAV was lost).

Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that belongs to a different
account returns `404` with the same shape as a nonexistent
sid — no enumeration leak.

See [docs/aws-recordings.md](https://github.com/voicetel/callBroadcast/blob/main/docs/aws-recordings.md)
for the archival pipeline.


Returns `audio/wav` binary content, or **302** with a presigned S3 URL in `Location`.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `recordings.fetchAudio`

item.name: IncomingPhoneNumbers
item.description: Tenant self-serve phone numbers — list, create, fetch, update, and delete DIDs assigned to the account.

query.description: Zero-based page index.
Page size. Max 1000; default 50.
The page token. This is provided by the API.

Exact-match filter on the E.164 phone number. Twilio's
canonical lookup pattern is
`GET /IncomingPhoneNumbers?PhoneNumber=+1...` followed by
`GET /IncomingPhoneNumbers/{sid}` using the `sid` from the
list result. Twilio-compatible SDK clients issue this
pattern; the filter is supported so the same call pattern
works against VoiceML. Returns a 0-or-1-row envelope.

Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML doesn't model per-DID
FriendlyName on phone_numbers (the field is empty in every
view), so this parameter is accepted but does not narrow
or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.

Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML does not model the
carrier-supplied "beta" flag (no upstream beta-status
distinction), so this parameter is accepted but does not
narrow or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.

Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML treats every assigned DID
as locally provisioned (no twilio/hosted origin
distinction), so this parameter is accepted but does not
narrow or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.

request.description:
**List DIDs assigned to the authenticated tenant**
Returns every DID currently bound to the authenticated
account. Tenant-scoped — only the caller's own rows are
returned; the global admin list endpoint is on the private
listener and not described here.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `incomingPhoneNumbers.list`

**Assign a DID to the authenticated tenant**
Tenant self-serve assignment of an E.164 DID. Idempotent on
the same tenant — re-POSTing the same `PhoneNumber` rebinds
its voice routing without erroring (matches Twilio's update
semantics). Returns 409 when the number is already claimed
by a different account.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `incomingPhoneNumbers.create`

**Fetch a single DID assigned to the authenticated tenant**
Tenant-scoped — a phone number that exists but belongs to a
different account 404s with the same shape as a nonexistent
number (no enumeration leak).


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `incomingPhoneNumbers.fetch`

**Update voice routing on an assigned DID**
Only-set-fields-touched semantics. Tenant-scoped — a number
assigned to a different account 404s.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `incomingPhoneNumbers.update`

**Release a DID from the authenticated tenant**
Tenant-scoped — a number assigned to a different account 404s
without affecting the row. Idempotent: 204 on success OR if
the number was already gone.


Returns **204 No Content** on success — no response body.

HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `incomingPhoneNumbers.delete`

item.name: Diagnostics
item.description: VoiceML extension endpoints — health probe and OpenAPI self-publish (no authentication required).

request.description:
**List available daily event-log archives (VoiceML extension)**
VoiceML extension (not a Twilio resource). Lists the authenticated
account's nightly event-log archives — calls, messages, recordings
metadata, and payments — available for self-service download.
Archives are produced once per day and retained for one year, after
which an S3 lifecycle rule deletes them.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `exports.list`

**Download a daily event-log archive (VoiceML extension)**
VoiceML extension (not a Twilio resource). Redirects (302) to a
time-limited presigned URL for the account's archive on the given
date.


HTTP Basic Auth: username = `{{accountSid}}`, password = `{{apiKey}}`.

OperationId: `exports.download`

**Deep liveness probe**
Composite probe with two-tier severity. Hard checks (storage,
disk free space, voice stack processes + required modules +
SIP profile state) flip the LB-facing `ok` boolean. Soft
checks (host CPU loadavg, memory pressure, workload percents,
cluster health) surface in `warnings` only — the LB stays in
rotation. Result cached 5 s on full success.


No authentication required.

OperationId: `health.check`
